analysis_summary.description: Field layout analysis for BerserkHandler and OnElementKilledHandler skill effect handlers

OnTargetHit.purpose: Grants bonus action points when target is killed

Create.purpose: Factory method to create BerserkHandler instance

Constructor.purpose: Initializes Berserk effect data with default values

effect_data_fields.name: BonusActionPoints
effect_data_fields.description: Number of bonus action points granted on kill

effect_data_fields.name: RemoveAfterTrigger
effect_data_fields.description: If true, removes the skill after triggering (one-time use)

kill_detection.description: param_4+0x4d contains kill confirmation flag (byte, non-zero = kill)

OnTargetHit.purpose: Reduces suppression when target is killed

ReduceSuppression.purpose: Helper method to apply suppression reduction to actor

Create.purpose: Factory method to create OnElementKilledHandler instance

Constructor.purpose: Initializes OnElementKilled effect data with default values

effect_data_fields.name: ReduceSuppressionType
effect_data_fields.description: Type of suppression reduction behavior (0 = OnKill type)

effect_data_fields.name: MaxSuppReduction
effect_data_fields.description: Maximum suppression points to reduce on kill

effect_data_fields.name: ShowTextOnSuppReduction
effect_data_fields.description: If true, displays text/icon when suppression is reduced

kill_detection.description: param_4+0x4c contains kill confirmation flag (byte, non-zero = kill)

0x10.description: Reference to the skill this handler belongs to

GetActor.description: Returns Actor associated with this handler's skill

GetEntity.description: Returns Entity associated with this handler's skill

key_discoveries.details: Both handlers store effect data reference at offset 0x18. The effect data classes (Berserk, OnElementKilled) inherit from BaseEffect which inherits from UnityEngine TileBase.
BerserkHandler checks param_4+0x4d for kill flag, while OnElementKilledHandler checks param_4+0x4c. This suggests different event parameter structures.
OnElementKilledHandler uses XOR with DAT_182d8fbb0 (likely 0x80000000) to negate float for suppression reduction - standard IEEE 754 sign bit flip.
After reducing suppression, OnElementKilledHandler sets a dirty flag at SkillContainer+0x59 to trigger UI/state updates.
Both use factory Create methods that allocate handler via il2cpp_object_new and store effect data reference, following Unity's ScriptableObject pattern.
BerserkHandler uses vtable+0x418/0x420 for target validation and vtable+0x458/0x460 for getting current action points.
Berserk grants 20 bonus AP by default, while OnElementKilled reduces up to 10 suppression points - these are baseline balance values.
